title: The Notice
description: Opening scene: The conscription notice at the Sleepy Slime Inn, party introductions.

lines: The notice was nailed to the door of the Sleepy Slime Inn at dawn, which was unusual, because nothing in Harmonia happened at dawn.
Things in Harmonia happened at a responsible mid-morning hour, after porridge and before the first ale.

lines.text: BY ORDER OF THE CROWN: All able-bodied citizens are hereby conscripted into auxiliary service for the defense of the realm.
Report to Elder Theron at the village square. Sincerely, The Royal Bureau of Definitely Real Military Operations.

lines: Nobody in Harmonia had ever seen a Royal Bureau of anything.
The village was six houses, a chapel, a shop that sold swords it had never tested, and an inn whose slime mascot had been repainted so many times it now resembled a vaguely threatening cloud.
But the notice was there. And the monsters were getting worse. And the real army was busy in the capital doing whatever it was armies did when chancellors needed protecting.
So four people showed up. Not because they were brave. Not because they were special. Because they were there, and nobody else was.

lines.text: The Fighter came because he had heard there was trouble, and trouble was what fighters were for. He had a sword he'd bought from the untested shop and convictions he'd polished to a shine.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: If there's trouble, I'll stand in front of it. That's... what I do.
The Cleric came because the chapel had been empty for a week and God, or whatever heard her prayers, had clearly decided someone should investigate.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: A calling is a calling. You don't second-guess it. You just... go where it points.
The Mage came because the pattern he'd found in the monster attacks was too interesting to ignore. Self-taught, mostly wrong about academic names, and completely correct about practical applications.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The attack frequency follows a geometric progression. Someone is designing these encounters. I want to know who.
The Rogue came because Elder Theron had seen a figure sleeping under the inn's eaves and pointed, and it had been that kind of morning.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: I was passing through. Made the critical error of still being here.
And the Bard came because the Bard went everywhere there was a story.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: A party of five! A valiant fighter, a servant of the cloth, a scholar of arcane learning, a rogue of disputed virtue, and a humble bard whose chronicles will outlast you all. What a tale this shall be!

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: What a tale it shall be... if you don't stop talking about it and actually help carry something.
